question_text: A 4 kg box is pushed across a rough floor. Four forces act on it: a pushing force of 25 N to the right, friction of 15 N to the left, its weight of 40 N downwards, and a normal reaction force from the floor. Calculate the magnitude of the normal reaction force and the net horizontal force. Then state whether the box will accelerate or remain at rest.
correct_answer: Normal reaction force is 40 N upwards. Net horizontal force is 10 N to the right. The box will accelerate to the right because there is an unbalanced horizontal force.
explanation: Vertically, the weight (40 N down) must be balanced by the normal reaction (40 N up) for vertical equilibrium. Horizontally, the pushing force (25 N right) minus friction (15 N left) gives a net force of 10 N to the right, which is unbalanced, so the box accelerates in that direction.
